comments.author: @OSAMAALYAFAAI
comments.text:
A huge thank you to True Globe for this magnificent and fair documentary! Thank you for showing the world the true face of Yemen—a land of rich history, deep-rooted civilization, and breathtaking nature that stands timeless. ❤️🇾🇪

​Heartfelt thanks to everyone in the comments section for your kind words and genuine feelings. Your beautiful responses truly reflect your noble character.

​We want to assure everyone that despite all challenges, Yemen remains a land of goodness and peace. Peace and tranquility will fully restore every corner of this beautiful country, God willing, and its warm-hearted people will always hold onto their values of hospitality and generosity.

​A warm welcome to all visitors and travelers from around the globe. Our hearts and homes are always open to you—Yemen is your second home! 🌹✨

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text:
Thank you for pointing that out. You’re right — eating with the right hand is an important part of Islamic culture and daily etiquette in many Muslim communities. We appreciate this kind of cultural detail because it helps make the story more accurate and respectful.
Are there any other daily customs or traditions you think should be shown more clearly in videos about Muslim countries?

comments.author: @WorldDiscovered_Official
comments.text: I still remember the silence of Yemen’s old villages, the warm tones of the mud houses, and the slow, simple rhythm of daily life. The people were kind and welcoming. I hope I can visit again someday in the near future.

replies.author: @OSAMAALYAFAAI
replies.text: Reading your comment brought so much joy to our hearts. It takes a noble soul to remember the warmth of our mud houses and the simple rhythm of our daily life so beautifully. Yemen remembers its friends, and we are eagerly waiting to welcome you back with open arms in the near future. Safe travels wherever you are! 🌹✨
